senses.definition: to repeatedly check what time it is because time seems to pass slowly and one is bored or tired

examples.x: I watch the clock closely to ensure I finish my work before the deadline.
She watches the clock during the meeting to make sure it doesn't run over time.
We watched the clock anxiously, hoping the bus wouldn't be delayed any further.
The students watched the clock intently, waiting for the bell to signal the end of the class.
The team will watch the clock closely in the final minutes of the game.

senses.definition: in a way that is instant and has no delay

examples.x: She handles customer inquiries on the spot, providing immediate assistance.
The team makes important decisions on the spot to navigate through unexpected challenges.
He responded on the spot when faced with a difficult question during the interview.
The firefighter acted heroically on the spot to rescue the trapped individuals.
The chef cooked a delicious meal on the spot to cater to the dietary preferences of the guests.
The emergency response team is providing medical aid on the spot to the injured victims.

senses.definition: used to convey the idea that time passes quickly or seems to move swiftly

examples.x: As we caught up over coffee, reminiscing about our school days, Jane remarked, "Time flies; it feels like just yesterday we were in high school."
After finishing the novel in one sitting, Sarah realized it was already midnight and exclaimed, "Wow, time flies when you're lost in a good book!"
Attending the reunion, Tom remarked to his old friends, "Seeing everyone after so long, it's hard to believe how time flies."
Looking at old family photos, Grandma smiled and said, "Time flies, doesn't it?
She is always ready to help her friends at the drop of a hat.
He volunteers for any opportunity that arises at the drop of a hat.
We traveled together often, and she would join me at the drop of a dime.
The team sprang into action at the drop of a hat when the alarm sounded.
He supported his friend's business venture at the drop of a dime.
She will be there to support you at the drop of a hat when you need her.
She is currently working on multiple projects and ready to take on new ones at the drop of a hat.
She always completes her work in a trice, never wasting a moment.
They respond to emails in a trice, ensuring quick communication with clients.
He finishes his daily workout routine in a trice, maintaining his fitness goals.
We resolved the issue in a trice, working together to find a solution.
The team completed the project in a trice, meeting the tight deadline.
They will handle any unexpected challenges in a trice, adapting quickly to new circumstances.
She will organize the event logistics in a trice, making sure everything runs smoothly.

senses.definition: soon or in a quick manner

examples.x: She can whip up a delicious meal in two shakes, impressing everyone with her culinary skills.
They respond to emails in two shakes, ensuring efficient communication with clients.
He fixes technical issues in two shakes, resolving problems swiftly.
We completed the project in two shakes, surprising everyone with our efficiency.
The team resolved the issue in two shakes, preventing any further disruptions.
They will handle any unexpected challenges in two shakes, adapting quickly to new circumstances.
They finish their tasks in two shakes of a lamb's tail, ensuring efficient productivity.
We completed the project in two shakes of a lamb's tail, surprising everyone with our speed and efficiency.
The team resolved the issue in two shakes of a lamb's tail, preventing any further complications.

senses.definition: used to describe something, typically an action or change, that is done without any difficulty or hesitation

examples.x: She dribbles past defenders on a dime, displaying her exceptional agility on the basketball court.
They change tactics on a dime, adapting their strategy to the evolving situation.
He stops his car on a dime, avoiding a collision with impressive reflexes.
She halted her bike on a dime to avoid hitting a pedestrian crossing the street.
He plans to execute his dance routine, changing movements on a dime to captivate the audience.
They are evading tackles on a dime, showcasing their agility on the football field.
We were marveling at their flawless synchronization as they danced on a dime, moving in perfect harmony.
She was spinning around on a dime, showcasing her figure skating skills with grace.

senses.definition: used to mean something happens or is done immediately

examples.x: They launch their marketing campaign out of the gate, generating immediate buzz.
The team kicked off the project right out of the gate, establishing a solid foundation for success.
They will be implementing their new strategy out of the gate, aiming for immediate results.

senses.definition: accomplishing or achieving something in one decisive action or with a single effort

examples.x: They implement the new policy at a stroke, revolutionizing the company's approach.
The government enacted the reform at a single stroke, bringing about sweeping changes to the healthcare system.
He plans to launch the product at a stroke, capturing a significant market share in one fell swoop.

senses.definition: used to suggest that right now is the only time a person has a chance for taking an opportunity or else they lose it forever

examples.x: She realizes that it's now or never to pursue her dream of starting her own business.
They decide it's now or never to ask their boss for a promotion.
He understands that it's now or never to confess his feelings to his longtime crush.
We knew it was now or never to submit our application for the competition.
She recognized it was now or never to take the leap and travel the world.
They will realize it's now or never to make a career change and pursue their passion.
Submit your application now or never to secure a spot in the prestigious program.

senses.definition: used to mean that by passing time it will be proved if something is true, valid, or effective

examples.x: We are uncertain about the outcome, but time will tell whether our efforts will be successful.
He is unsure about the future, but time will tell which path he should take.
They will be anxious to see the results, but time will tell if their hard work paid off.

senses.definition: in a manner that is sudden, unexpected, and not gradual

examples.x: He quits smoking cold turkey, without any nicotine replacement therapy.
She stops drinking alcohol cold turkey, choosing to quit without any gradual reduction.
They give up fast food cold turkey, eliminating it completely from their diet.
He quit gambling cold turkey, cutting off all access to casinos and online betting.
They gave up sugary drinks cold turkey, opting for healthier alternatives instead.

word_list.pronunciation: ɪnðə blˈɪŋk ɔːɹ twˈɪŋkəl əvən ˈaɪ

examples.x: He dodges the punch in the blink of an eye, showcasing his lightning-fast reflexes.
He grabbed the opportunity in the blink of an eye, making a decision without hesitation.
He was responding in the blink of an eye, showing his ability to handle unexpected challenges.
They respond to emergencies in the twinkle of an eye, showing their efficiency and quick thinking.

senses.definition: in an instant and with no delay

examples.x: She knew the answer to the question right off the bat.
He made a decision off the bat without much thought.
The team scored a goal off the bat, surprising their opponents.
The teacher recognized the correct solution off the bat.
They agreed to the terms of the contract off the bat.

senses.definition: a long day that is unproductive and boring

examples.x: It's been a really slow day at the shop so far with very few customers coming in.
Yesterday was such a slow day at the office that I ended up leaving early.
From the lack of reservations on the books, it looks like it's going to be a slow day for us this Saturday.
It had been an unusually slow day, with not a single service call by mid-afternoon.
Seeing how few tables we've seated so far, it's looking like it's going to be a slow day here at the restaurant.
By closing time, it will have been a slow day if business doesn't pick up soon.
Things would be less hectic now if it had been a slow day workload-wise like we expected.

senses.definition: used to refer to a process that occurs slowly or step by step over time, rather than happening suddenly or all at once

examples.x: He is improving his guitar skills by degrees, practicing every day and gradually becoming more proficient.
She is building her business by degrees, taking small steps and steadily expanding her customer base.
They are repairing the old house by degrees, tackling one room at a time and slowly restoring it to its former glory.
He improved his fitness level by degrees, starting with short workouts and gradually increasing the intensity over time.
She built her career by degrees, taking on new responsibilities and climbing the corporate ladder step by step.

word_list.pronunciation: bɪfˌoːɹ ˌɛsbˈiː kæn ɔːɹ kʊd sˈeɪ dʒˈæk ɹˈɑːbɪnsən

senses.definition: so suddenly or quickly that leaves one with no time to react or notice anything

examples.x: They react to changes before you can say Jack Robinson, adapting instantly to new circumstances.
He finished the race before you could say Jack Robinson, crossing the finish line in a flash.
They resolved the issue before anyone could say Jack Robinson, finding a quick and effective solution.
